`use` import (grouped `{{a, b}}`) — the transpiler has no cross-nodule symbol table, so it cannot confirm the imported path resolves to a declared Mycelium nodule; the M-1000 vet loop confirms such imports fail `myc check` name-resolution (a Rust `use extern_crate::Sym` names a crate, not a nodule). Flagged, not emitted — the same flag-don't-guess stance `map_type`/`emit_expr` take on qualified paths/calls (DN-34 §4/§8.2; VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The content hash of a runtime *value*: its identity-bearing `Repr` + payload, with all dynamic"] # [doc = " [`Meta`](mycelium_core::Meta) excluded (RFC-0001 §4.6)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = " A content hash is a pure function of the value's normalized structure (RFC-0001 §4.6 WF4)."] # [doc = " Two values with identical `Repr` + payload but different provenance/bounds **collide**"] # [doc = " (same hash — correct, metadata is not identity — ADR-003). Two values differing in `Repr`"] # [doc = " get different hashes (paradigm is identity-bearing — RFC-0001 §4.6)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [doc = " Every value has an identity; this op cannot fail. (C1: no failure mode to hide.)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [doc = " Pure computation; no IO, time, randomness, or allocation beyond the hash output."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG: Q2"] # [doc = " If the kernel adds a refusal path for mixed-paradigm composite values, this signature will"] # [doc = " change to `Result<ContentHash, IdentityRefusal>`. See module-level FLAG Q2."] # [must_use] pub fn hash_of_value (v : & mycelium_core :: Value) -> ContentHash { v . content_hash () }
gaps.reason: qualified type path `mycelium_core :: Value` — collapsing to its last segment would risk colliding with an unrelated same-named local type (e.g. `std::cmp::Ordering` vs a local `Ordering`); left an explicit gap rather than guessed (VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The content hash of a definition (hash-of-AST; RFC-0001 §4.6 `hash(def)`):"] # [doc = " ```text"] # [doc = " H( normalize(structure(def)) ‖ types_with_repr(def) ‖ static_contract(def) )"] # [doc = " ```"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = " Identity is over the α-normalized structure, types-with-`Repr`, constant literals, operator"] # [doc = " names, and swap contracts — **never** over binder names or dynamic value metadata (ADR-003)."] # [doc = " Consequences (RFC-0001 §4.6 acceptance):"] # [doc = " - Trivial renames do **not** change the hash (α-renaming a binder is not identity)."] # [doc = " - Identical definitions **collide** (same hash — correct, not a bug)."] # [doc = " - A paradigm change **does** change the hash (types include `Repr`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [doc = " Every definition node has a content identity; this op cannot fail. (C1.)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [must_use] pub fn hash_of_def (d : & mycelium_core :: Node) -> ContentHash { d . content_hash () }
gaps.reason: qualified type path `mycelium_core :: Node` — collapsing to its last segment would risk colliding with an unrelated same-named local type (e.g. `std::cmp::Ordering` vs a local `Ordering`); left an explicit gap rather than guessed (VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Identity equality by digest: two content hashes are **the same identity** iff their digests"] # [doc = " are equal."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = " Digest comparison is a pure byte-equality test; it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [doc = " Comparing two digests cannot fail."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Note"] # [doc = " This is *not* the same as structural `==` on the values themselves: two values with"] # [doc = " different runtime state but the same normalized content (e.g. differing only in provenance)"] # [doc = " are `digest_eq` (same identity) even if `v1 != v2` structurally (ADR-003)."] # [must_use] pub fn digest_eq (a : & ContentHash , b : & ContentHash) -> bool { a == b }

# [doc = " Build a typed [`ContentRef`] that cert / policy / provenance / `spore` artifacts embed to"] # [doc = " designate a content-addressed target."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The `kind` parameter carries the role of the reference (`Value`, `Def`, `Policy`, …) so"] # [doc = " downstream modules can distinguish roles without parsing the digest (no black boxes, C3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = " A pure construction from a known kind and hash; total and effect-free."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [doc = " There is no failure mode."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [must_use] pub fn as_ref (h : ContentHash , kind : RefKind) -> ContentRef { ContentRef :: new (kind , h) }
gaps.reason: qualified/associated-function call `ContentRef :: new` — no established Mycelium surface form for a Rust conversion-op body; emitting the bare last-segment name would fabricate a call (e.g. `from(...)` is not a Mycelium builtin)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Parse a content-address string (`<algo>:<digest>`) into a [`ContentHash`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = " If the string is well-formed, the result is always the same for the same input."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility: `Err(MalformedDigest)` on bad shape (C1 — never-silent)"] # [doc = " A string that does not match the `<algo>:<digest>` pattern returns"] # [doc = " `Err(`[`MalformedDigest`]`)`, carrying:"] # [doc = " - the rejected input (so the caller can surface it), and"] # [doc = " - a description of *why* it was rejected (G11 dual projection)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A zeroed / synthetic / sentinel digest is **never** returned for malformed input (C1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] pub fn parse_ref (s : & str) -> Result < ContentHash , MalformedDigest > { ContentHash :: parse (s) . ok_or_else (| | { let description = if ! s . contains (':') { "missing ':' separator between algo and digest" } else { let (algo , digest) = s . split_once (':') . expect ("contains ':'") ; if algo . is_empty () { "algo part is empty" } else if digest . is_empty () { "digest part is empty" } else if ! algo . bytes () . all (| b | b . is_ascii_lowercase () || b . is_ascii_digit ()) { "algo must be [a-z0-9]+" } else { "digest must be [A-Za-z0-9_-]+" } } ; MalformedDigest :: new (s , description) }) }
gaps.reason: `String`/`str` — `Bytes` exists in base_type but is not confirmed equivalent to a UTF-8 text type

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Look up the name bound to a content hash in `registry`, returning `None` when the name is"] # [doc = " unbound."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = " A pure read of the name registry."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility: `None` when name unbound (C1 — never-silent)"] # [doc = " An unbound name is an honest `None`, **never** a sentinel hash or a fabricated name (C1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [doc = " The name registry is a content-addressed, append-only side-table; reads are effect-free."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG: Q3"] # [doc = " See module-level FLAG Q3 on registry ownership."] # [must_use] pub fn resolve_name < 'r > (registry : & 'r NameRegistry , hash : & ContentHash) -> Option < & 'r str > { registry . resolve_name (hash) }
gaps.reason: lifetime parameter `'r` has no grammar surface

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " All names bound to `hash` in `registry`, as a list (0 or 1 entries with the current kernel;"] # [doc = " see [`name_registry`] module-level FLAG on the one-name limitation)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns an empty `Vec` when no name is bound — **never** a sentinel (C1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = " A pure read of the name registry."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total (possibly empty list)"] # [doc = " The op cannot fail; an unbound hash yields an empty list (not an error)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG: Q3"] # [doc = " See module-level FLAG Q3 on registry ownership."] # [must_use] pub fn names_of (registry : & NameRegistry , hash : & ContentHash) -> Vec < String > { registry . names_of (hash) }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { # ! [doc = " Integration-level tests over the public API surface."] # ! [doc = ""] # ! [doc = " These tests assert the *behavioural* guarantees stated in the guarantee matrix and spec §4."] # ! [doc = " The guarantee matrix itself is unit-tested in [`crate::guarantee_matrix::tests`]."] # ! [doc = ""] # ! [doc = " # Randomised identity / collision tests"] # ! [doc = " No sibling crate uses proptest, so we use randomised std tests (spec instruction: \"else"] # ! [doc = " randomized std tests\"). The randomised tests use a fixed seed (noted in each test) so they"] # ! [doc = " are reproducible; the seed is also the \"mutant witness\" (dev-workflow banked guard #7)."] use super :: * ; use mycelium_core :: { meta :: { Meta , Provenance } , node :: Node , repr :: Repr , value :: { Payload , Value } , } ; fn byte_value (bits : [bool ; 8]) -> Value { Value :: new (Repr :: Binary { width : 8 } , Payload :: Bits (bits . to_vec ()) , Meta :: exact (Provenance :: Root) ,) . expect ("well-formed byte value") } const BITS_A : [bool ; 8] = [true , false , true , true , false , false , true , false] ; const BITS_B : [bool ; 8] = [false , true , false , false , true , true , false , true] ; fn const_node (bits : [bool ; 8]) -> Node { Node :: Const (byte_value (bits)) } fn let_node (binder : & str) -> Node { Node :: Let { id : binder . to_owned () , bound : Box :: new (const_node (BITS_A)) , body : Box :: new (Node :: Var (binder . to_owned ())) , } } # [doc = " `hash_of_value` is deterministic: the same value always yields the same hash."] # [doc = " Guard: any randomness in `hash_of_value` makes this fail."] # [test] fn hash_of_value_is_deterministic () { let v = byte_value (BITS_A) ; assert_eq ! (hash_of_value (& v) , hash_of_value (& v)) ; } # [doc = " Two values with identical `Repr` + payload collide regardless of `Meta` (ADR-003)."] # [doc = " Guard: hashing Meta in `hash_of_value` makes this fail."] # [test] fn hash_of_value_excludes_meta () { let exact_meta = byte_value (BITS_A) ; let derived_meta = Value :: new (Repr :: Binary { width : 8 } , Payload :: Bits (BITS_A . to_vec ()) , Meta :: new (Provenance :: Derived { op : ContentHash :: parse ("blake3:someop") . unwrap () , inputs : vec ! [] , } , mycelium_core :: GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , None , None , None , None ,) . expect ("valid meta") ,) . expect ("well-formed") ; assert ! (digest_eq (& hash_of_value (& exact_meta) , & hash_of_value (& derived_meta)) , "values differing only in Meta must collide (ADR-003 — metadata is not identity)") ; } # [doc = " Values differing in payload get different hashes."] # [doc = " Guard: truncating the payload in `hash_of_value` makes this fail."] # [test] fn hash_of_value_different_payloads_differ () { assert ! (! digest_eq (& hash_of_value (& byte_value (BITS_A)) , & hash_of_value (& byte_value (BITS_B)) ,)) ; } # [doc = " Values differing in `Repr` paradigm get different hashes (RFC-0001 §4.6)."] # [doc = " Guard: excluding Repr from `hash_of_value` makes this fail."] # [test] fn hash_of_value_different_repr_differ () { let binary = byte_value (BITS_A) ; let ternary = Value :: new (Repr :: Ternary { trits : 6 } , Payload :: Trits (vec ! [mycelium_core :: value :: Trit :: Zero ; 6]) , Meta :: exact (Provenance :: Root) ,) . expect ("well-formed ternary") ; assert ! (! digest_eq (& hash_of_value (& binary) , & hash_of_value (& ternary) ,)) ; } # [doc = " `hash_of_def` is deterministic."] # [doc = " Guard: any randomness in `hash_of_def` makes this fail."] # [test] fn hash_of_def_is_deterministic () { let n = let_node ("x") ; assert_eq ! (hash_of_def (& n) , hash_of_def (& n)) ; } # [doc = " α-renaming a binder does NOT change identity (RFC-0001 §4.6; ADR-003)."] # [doc = " Guard: including binder names in the hash makes this fail."] # [test] fn hash_of_def_alpha_renaming_preserves_identity () { assert ! (digest_eq (& hash_of_def (& let_node ("x")) , & hash_of_def (& let_node ("long_name"))) , "α-renaming a binder must not change identity (RFC-0001 §4.6)") ; } # [doc = " Identical definitions collide."] # [doc = " Guard: any non-determinism in `hash_of_def` makes this fail."] # [test] fn hash_of_def_identical_defs_collide () { assert ! (digest_eq (& hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) , & hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) ,)) ; } # [doc = " Distinct definitions get different hashes."] # [doc = " Guard: any collision in `hash_of_def` beyond the correct ones makes this fail."] # [test] fn hash_of_def_distinct_defs_differ () { assert ! (! digest_eq (& hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) , & hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_B)) ,)) ; } # [doc = " A paradigm change changes identity (RFC-0001 §4.6)."] # [doc = " Guard: excluding Repr from the node hash makes this fail."] # [test] fn hash_of_def_paradigm_change_changes_identity () { let bin_node = const_node (BITS_A) ; let tern_node = Node :: Const (Value :: new (Repr :: Ternary { trits : 6 } , Payload :: Trits (vec ! [mycelium_core :: value :: Trit :: Zero ; 6]) , Meta :: exact (Provenance :: Root) ,) . unwrap () ,) ; assert ! (! digest_eq (& hash_of_def (& bin_node) , & hash_of_def (& tern_node) ,)) ; } # [doc = " `digest_eq` is reflexive."] # [doc = " Guard: returning false for a==a makes this fail."] # [test] fn digest_eq_is_reflexive () { let h = hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) ; assert ! (digest_eq (& h , & h)) ; } # [doc = " `digest_eq` is symmetric."] # [doc = " Guard: an asymmetric comparison makes this fail."] # [test] fn digest_eq_is_symmetric () { let h1 = hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) ; let h2 = hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) ; assert_eq ! (digest_eq (& h1 , & h2) , digest_eq (& h2 , & h1)) ; } # [doc = " `digest_eq` is transitive."] # [doc = " Guard: a non-transitive equality makes this fail."] # [test] fn digest_eq_is_transitive () { let h1 = hash_of_def (& let_node ("a")) ; let h2 = hash_of_def (& let_node ("b")) ; let h3 = hash_of_def (& let_node ("c")) ; if digest_eq (& h1 , & h2) && digest_eq (& h2 , & h3) { assert ! (digest_eq (& h1 , & h3) , "digest_eq must be transitive") ; } } # [doc = " `as_ref` wraps the hash with the given kind; the kind is inspectable (C3: no black boxes)."] # [test] fn as_ref_preserves_kind_and_hash () { let h = hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) ; let r = as_ref (h . clone () , RefKind :: Def) ; assert_eq ! (r . kind () , RefKind :: Def) ; assert_eq ! (r . hash () , & h) ; } # [doc = " Two refs with the same hash but different kinds are not equal (kind is identity-bearing for"] # [doc = " the ref, not the target)."] # [doc = " Guard: ignoring kind in ContentRef equality makes this fail."] # [test] fn as_ref_kind_is_part_of_ref_identity () { let h = hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) ; let value_ref = as_ref (h . clone () , RefKind :: Value) ; let def_ref = as_ref (h . clone () , RefKind :: Def) ; assert_ne ! (value_ref , def_ref , "kind must be part of ContentRef identity") ; } # [doc = " `parse_ref` accepts well-formed addresses."] # [test] fn parse_ref_accepts_well_formed () { assert ! (parse_ref ("blake3:Hh3kQ_x-1A") . is_ok ()) ; assert ! (parse_ref ("sha256:abcdef0123456789") . is_ok ()) ; } # [doc = " `parse_ref` rejects malformed addresses with a typed error (C1 — never-silent)."] # [doc = " The error carries both the rejected input and a description (G11)."] # [doc = " Guard: returning Ok for malformed input makes this fail."] # [test] fn parse_ref_rejects_malformed_with_explicit_error () { for bad in & ["no-colon" , "blake3:" , ":digest" , "UPPER:abc" , "blake3:has space" ,] { let err = parse_ref (bad) . expect_err ("must reject malformed input") ; assert_eq ! (& err . input , bad , "error must carry the rejected input") ; assert ! (! err . description . is_empty () , "error must carry a description (G11)") ; } } # [doc = " `parse_ref` returns a hash that round-trips back to the original string."] # [test] fn parse_ref_round_trips () { let s = "blake3:Hh3kQ_x-1A" ; let h = parse_ref (s) . expect ("well-formed") ; assert_eq ! (h . as_str () , s) ; } # [doc = " The error description disambiguates *which* rule is violated (G11 dual projection)."] # [doc = " Guard: returning the same generic description for all errors makes this fail."] # [test] fn parse_ref_error_description_is_specific () { let missing_colon = parse_ref ("nocolon") . unwrap_err () ; let empty_algo = parse_ref (":digest") . unwrap_err () ; assert_ne ! (missing_colon . description , empty_algo . description , "error descriptions must distinguish rule violations (G11)") ; } # [doc = " `resolve_name` returns `None` for an unbound hash (C1 — not a sentinel)."] # [test] fn resolve_name_none_for_unbound () { let reg = NameRegistry :: new () ; let h = hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) ; assert_eq ! (resolve_name (& reg , & h) , None) ; } # [doc = " `names_of` returns an empty list for an unbound hash (C1 — not a sentinel)."] # [test] fn names_of_empty_for_unbound () { let reg = NameRegistry :: new () ; let h = hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) ; assert ! (names_of (& reg , & h) . is_empty ()) ; } # [doc = " Binding a name and resolving it round-trips."] # [test] fn resolve_name_and_names_of_after_bind () { let mut reg = NameRegistry :: new () ; let h = hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) ; reg . bind (h . clone () , "my_const") ; assert_eq ! (resolve_name (& reg , & h) , Some ("my_const")) ; assert_eq ! (names_of (& reg , & h) , vec ! ["my_const" . to_owned ()]) ; } # [doc = " Binding a name does not change the hash (ADR-003 — metadata is not identity)."] # [doc = " Guard: any hash mutation on bind makes this fail."] # [test] fn bind_does_not_change_hash_identity () { let mut reg = NameRegistry :: new () ; let h_before = hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) ; reg . bind (h_before . clone () , "before") ; let h_after = hash_of_def (& const_node (BITS_A)) ; assert ! (digest_eq (& h_before , & h_after) , "binding a name must not change the hash (ADR-003)") ; } # [doc = " Randomised: 256 distinct single-byte payloads all get distinct hashes (collision test)."] # [doc = " One deterministic sample of 256 (all possible 8-bit values)."] # [test] fn hash_of_def_all_single_byte_payloads_are_distinct () { let mut hashes : std :: collections :: HashSet < String > = std :: collections :: HashSet :: new () ; for i in 0u8 ..= 255 { let bits : [bool ; 8] = [(i >> 7) & 1 == 1 , (i >> 6) & 1 == 1 , (i >> 5) & 1 == 1 , (i >> 4) & 1 == 1 , (i >> 3) & 1 == 1 , (i >> 2) & 1 == 1 , (i >> 1) & 1 == 1 , i & 1 == 1 ,] ; let h = hash_of_def (& const_node (bits)) ; assert ! (hashes . insert (h . as_str () . to_owned ()) , "byte {i} produced a hash collision — content hash must be injective") ; } assert_eq ! (hashes . len () , 256) ; } # [doc = " Randomised: `hash_of_value` is deterministic for 16 distinct payloads and each pair of"] # [doc = " identical-payload values produces the same hash (C4 / collision property)."] # [doc = " (One deterministic sample of 16, seed = 0xCA_FE_BA_BE_DE_AD_BE_EF.)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Design note — `hash_of_value` vs `hash_of_def(Const(v))`"] # [doc = " These are intentionally **different** hashes (domain-separated in the kernel): `hash_of_value`"] # [doc = " hashes the `repr + payload` of the value directly (the `Canon::value` path), while"] # [doc = " `hash_of_def(Node::Const(v))` prepends a `CONST` node tag so a constant node and a bare"] # [doc = " value cannot collide. Both are correct and `Exact`; they describe *different things* (a value"] # [doc = " vs a definition node containing that value). This test confirms determinism and collision"] # [doc = " semantics for `hash_of_value` without conflating the two."] # [test] fn hash_of_value_is_deterministic_and_collision_correct_for_16_samples () { let mut state : u64 = 0xCA_FE_BA_BE_DE_AD_BE_EF_u64 ; let mut seen : Vec < (u8 , ContentHash) > = Vec :: new () ; for _ in 0 .. 16 { state = state . wrapping_mul (6364136223846793005) . wrapping_add (1442695040888963407) ; let byte = (state >> 56) as u8 ; let bits : [bool ; 8] = [(byte >> 7) & 1 == 1 , (byte >> 6) & 1 == 1 , (byte >> 5) & 1 == 1 , (byte >> 4) & 1 == 1 , (byte >> 3) & 1 == 1 , (byte >> 2) & 1 == 1 , (byte >> 1) & 1 == 1 , byte & 1 == 1 ,] ; let v1 = byte_value (bits) ; let v2 = byte_value (bits) ; let h1 = hash_of_value (& v1) ; let h2 = hash_of_value (& v2) ; assert ! (digest_eq (& h1 , & h2) , "hash_of_value must be deterministic: identical values must collide (byte={byte:#010b})") ; seen . push ((byte , h1)) ; } for i in 0 .. seen . len () { for j in (i + 1) .. seen . len () { if seen [i] . 0 != seen [j] . 0 { assert ! (! digest_eq (& seen [i] . 1 , & seen [j] . 1) , "hash_of_value produced a collision for distinct bytes {} and {}" , seen [i] . 0 , seen [j] . 0) ; } } } } }
gaps.reason: #[cfg(test)] item — out of scope for this PoC's transpilation surface (excluded from the expressible-fraction denominator, but recorded, never silently skipped)
